title: Protocol acknowledgment (response.processed) and internal prewarm
purpose: Complete the WS protocol contract by sending response.processed after each completed response, and add the internal prewarm optimization that sends generate: false on fresh connections. Both are WS-specific protocol behaviors that sit on top of the request/response framing (PW-007).
task: After the WS receive loop produces ProviderEvent::Done, send {"type": "response.processed", "response_id": "..."} back on the WebSocket. Log failures at warn level but do not fail the request. Add an internal prewarm capability: the WS transport may send a generate: false request on fresh connections to prime the server's prompt cache. Prewarm is best-effort and internal — no method on the Provider trait.

requirements.title: Send response.processed after Done
requirements.spec: WHEN the WS transport receives a ProviderEvent::Done with a response_id THE SYSTEM SHALL send {"type": "response.processed", "response_id": "<id>"} on the WebSocket.
acceptance: response.processed message sent as WS Text after Done event
response_id matches the value from ProviderEvent::Done
Message sent before returning the connection to the pool

requirements.title: response.processed failure is non-fatal
requirements.spec: IF sending response.processed fails (connection dropped, write error) THE SYSTEM SHALL log the failure at warn level AND SHALL NOT propagate the error to the caller. The response was already successfully received.
acceptance: Send failure logged at tracing::warn with error details
Caller receives the complete ProviderEvent stream regardless of ack failure
Connection marked broken for the pool if the send failed due to connection loss

requirements.title: Internal prewarm on fresh connections
requirements.spec: The WS transport MAY send a request with generate: false internally on a fresh connection to prime the server's prompt cache. This is a transport-level optimization, not a caller-facing API.
acceptance: Prewarm sends the same payload structure as a real request with generate: false appended
Server response to prewarm is consumed and discarded
Prewarm is triggered by transport internals, not by callers

requirements.title: Prewarm is best-effort
requirements.spec: IF a prewarm fails or another caller takes the connection before the real request THE SYSTEM SHALL proceed with the real request normally. Prewarm failure SHALL NOT cause request failure.
acceptance: Failed prewarm does not prevent the real request from being sent
Connection stolen between prewarm and real request: caller falls back to another slot or HTTP
No error propagated to caller from prewarm failure

requirements.title: Provider trait unchanged
requirements.spec: The Provider trait SHALL NOT gain a prewarm method. The trait SHALL remain single-method (stream only).
acceptance: Provider trait in traits.rs has only fn stream()
No prewarm method on Provider, MockProvider, or any trait implementation

boundaries: SHALL NOT add prewarm to the Provider trait
SHALL NOT block the real request waiting for prewarm to complete if the connection is contested
SHALL NOT modify the ProviderEvent stream — response.processed is sent after the stream is complete
verification: Tests verify response.processed sent after Done event with correct response_id
Tests verify send failure logged at warn and does not affect caller
Provider trait grep confirms single stream method only
DebugDumper write_ws_send captures the response.processed message
